mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 2.8 percent forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.8 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of observations or shows a rank correlation below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms robust aggregate demand that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when either series contracts, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production.
